R Notebook

library(data.table)
library(Rtsne)
library(R.utils)
library(ggplot2)
library(ggcorrplot)
theme_set(theme_bw())

Processing data

Sade-Feldman(2018) data

fnm1 = "GSE120575_Sade_Feldman_melanoma_single_cells_nolabel_1.txt.gz"
fnm2 = "GSE120575_Sade_Feldman_melanoma_single_cells_2.txt.gz"

sf_tpm1 = fread(paste0("../../scRNAseq/Sade_Feldman_2018/", fnm1), 
                fill = TRUE, header = TRUE)

sf_tpm2 = fread(paste0("../../scRNAseq/Sade_Feldman_2018/", fnm2), 
                fill = TRUE, drop = 16293, col.names = colnames(sf_tpm1))

ls_tpm = list(sf_tpm1,sf_tpm2)
sf_tpm = rbindlist(ls_tpm)

rm(ls_tpm)
rm(sf_tpm1)
rm(sf_tpm2)
dim(sf_tpm) # 55737 x 16292
## [1] 55737 16292
gene.names = sf_tpm$V1
rownames(sf_tpm) = gene.names
sf_tpm$V1 = NULL

Select a subset of genes that are expressed in more than 500 cells, i.e., about 3% of the 16,292 cells.

gene.grouping = rep(1:28, each = 2000)[1:dim(sf_tpm)[1]]
cells.per.gene = NULL
for (i in 1:28) {
  temp.counts = rowSums(sf_tpm[gene.grouping==i,] > 0)
  cells.per.gene = c(cells.per.gene,temp.counts)
}
table(cells.per.gene==0)
## 
## FALSE  TRUE 
## 50513  5224
table(cells.per.gene==1)
## 
## FALSE  TRUE 
## 53323  2414
table(cells.per.gene > 100)
## 
## FALSE  TRUE 
## 35552 20185
table(cells.per.gene > 500)
## 
## FALSE  TRUE 
## 43032 12705
# kept 12705 genes
large.express.genes.all = gene.names[which(cells.per.gene > 500)] 
rm(gene.grouping)

11 cell types we defined from Sade-Feldman(2018) data

cell_type = read.table("./output/cell_type.txt", header = TRUE, sep = "\t", 
                       as.is = TRUE)
dim(cell_type)
## [1] 16291     4
cell_type[1:2,]
clustered_cells = cell_type$cell[cell_type$type != "unclustered"]
length(clustered_cells)
## [1] 14743
# select TPM data for 12705 genes with large expression in 14743 clustered cells
clustered_tpm_LEgenes = sf_tpm[match(large.express.genes.all, rownames(sf_tpm)), ..clustered_cells] 
rownames(clustered_tpm_LEgenes) = large.express.genes.all

dim(clustered_tpm_LEgenes)
## [1] 12705 14743
clustered_tpm_LEgenes[1:2,1:6]
rm(sf_tpm)

percentages of zero expression genes for each cell in each cell type

zeros.per.cell = colSums(clustered_tpm_LEgenes==0) / nrow(clustered_tpm_LEgenes)
zeros.per.cell[1:2]
## A10_P3_M11 A11_P1_M11 
##  0.8492719  0.8802046
ctype = factor(cell_type$type[match(clustered_cells, cell_type$cell)])
zero.percentages = data.table(percentage.zero = zeros.per.cell, grouping = ctype)
rownames(zero.percentages) = names(zeros.per.cell)
dim(zero.percentages)
## [1] 14743     2
head(zero.percentages)
# distribution of the percentages of zero expression genes for cells by cell type
ggplot(data = zero.percentages) + 
  geom_histogram(aes(x=percentage.zero), binwidth = 0.01) + 
  facet_wrap(~grouping) + 
  ggtitle("Distribution of the percentages of genes with zero expression")

zero.percentages[, .(.N), by = .(grouping)]
z2 = zero.percentages[, .(qs=list(quantile(percentage.zero))), 
                 by = .(grouping)]
print(z2)
##                  grouping                                                qs
##  1:                   NK2 0.7452184,0.8476978,0.8645415,0.8807556,0.9238095
##  2:                 Tregs 0.6525777,0.7932310,0.8170799,0.8391185,0.8927981
##  3:                   NK3 0.6965762,0.7841007,0.8132231,0.8375443,0.8916962
##  4:                CD8T_G 0.7765447,0.8503739,0.8670602,0.8829595,0.9161747
##  5:                CD8T_B 0.4697363,0.7688705,0.8077135,0.8312475,0.8860291
##  6:       Dendritic_cells 0.6597403,0.7287682,0.7619835,0.7907517,0.8772924
##  7:           CD4T_memory 0.7517513,0.8461236,0.8632822,0.8792601,0.9163322
##  8: Monocytes_Macrophages 0.3922078,0.6466745,0.7315230,0.7891381,0.9118457
##  9:                   NK1 0.4010232,0.6537584,0.6915388,0.7237702,0.8393546
## 10:          Plasma_cells 0.6342385,0.7905155,0.8169618,0.8480126,0.9160173
## 11:               B_cells 0.6573003,0.8397481,0.8613932,0.8772137,0.9127902
rm(zeros.per.cell)

Based on the graph, the spread of distribution of the percentages of zero expression genes is quite different for each cell type, here we try to select 200 cells with the smallest percentages of zero expression genes.

fewzero_cells = unname(unlist(by(zero.percentages, zero.percentages$grouping, 
                                 FUN = function(x) rownames(x)[order(x$percentage.zero)][1:200])))
fewzero_cells[1:3]
## [1] "E4_P4_M47" "E5_P4_M47" "C9_P1_M47"

LM22 signature matrix

  • select genes that appear both in the LM22 signature matrix and the SF data
  • in those genes, further select genes that have expression in a relatively large amount of cells
CBST_LM22 = read.table("../../_reference/LM22.txt", sep = "\t", header = TRUE, as.is = TRUE)
dim(CBST_LM22)
## [1] 547  23
CBST_LM22[1:2,]
# 538 out of 547
CBST.SFgenes = CBST_LM22$Gene.symbol[CBST_LM22$Gene.symbol %in% gene.names]
length(CBST.SFgenes)
## [1] 538
# 323 out of 538
CBST.largeExpression.genes = intersect(CBST.SFgenes, large.express.genes.all)
length(CBST.largeExpression.genes)
## [1] 323
# 323 x 23
CBST_SFLEgenes_LM22 = CBST_LM22[match(CBST.largeExpression.genes, CBST_LM22$Gene.symbol),] 

dim(CBST_SFLEgenes_LM22)
## [1] 323  23
CBST_SFLEgenes_LM22[1:2,]

Compare the correlation of cell types

  • Compare the correlation between our 11 cell types.
  • Compare the correlation between the cell types in the LM22 vs. the 11 cell types we had.
  • Two types of correlation: using all cells for each cell type to calculate mean gene expression, or calculate the mean expression using 200 cells with smallest percentages of zero expression genes.

Correlation matrix for our 11 cell types

using mean expression of filtered genes shared between SF data and LM22 matrix

table(rownames(clustered_tpm_LEgenes) == large.express.genes.all)
## 
##  TRUE 
## 12705
match1 = match(CBST.largeExpression.genes, large.express.genes.all)
LEgenes_CBST_tpm = as.data.table(t(clustered_tpm_LEgenes[match1,]))

rownames(LEgenes_CBST_tpm) = clustered_cells
colnames(LEgenes_CBST_tpm) = CBST.largeExpression.genes
dim(LEgenes_CBST_tpm)
## [1] 14743   323
LEgenes_CBST_tpm[1:5,1:5]
LEgenes_CBST_tpm$grouping = factor(cell_type$type[match(clustered_cells, cell_type$cell)])
table(LEgenes_CBST_tpm$grouping)
## 
##               B_cells           CD4T_memory                CD8T_B 
##                  1421                  1099                  2458 
##                CD8T_G       Dendritic_cells Monocytes_Macrophages 
##                  3033                   275                  1341 
##                   NK1                   NK2                   NK3 
##                   463                  1904                  1146 
##          Plasma_cells                 Tregs 
##                   290                  1313
cmean = LEgenes_CBST_tpm[, lapply(.SD, mean), by = grouping]
cmed  = LEgenes_CBST_tpm[, lapply(.SD, median), by = grouping]
csd   = LEgenes_CBST_tpm[, lapply(.SD, sd), by = grouping]

dim(cmean)
## [1]  11 324
cmean[1:2,1:5]
dim(cmed)
## [1]  11 324
cmed[1:2,1:5]
dim(csd)
## [1]  11 324
csd[1:2,1:5]
plot(as.numeric(unlist(cmean[,-1])), as.numeric(unlist(csd[,-1])), 
     xlab="mean expression per gene per cell type", 
     ylab="sd per gene per cell type")

plot(as.numeric(unlist(cmean[,-1])), as.numeric(unlist(cmed[,-1])), 
     xlab="mean expression per gene per cell type", 
     ylab="median expression per gene per cell type")

LEgene_CBST_expression = LEgenes_CBST_tpm[, lapply(.SD, mean), by = grouping] # 11 x 324
LEgene_CBST_expression = LEgene_CBST_expression[order(LEgene_CBST_expression$grouping),]
LEgene_CBST_expression[,1:6]
LEgene_CBST_compare_expression = as.data.frame(t(LEgene_CBST_expression[,-1]))
colnames(LEgene_CBST_compare_expression) = LEgene_CBST_expression$grouping
head(LEgene_CBST_compare_expression)
rm(LEgene_CBST_expression)
rm(LEgenes_CBST_tpm)

The patterns of the correlation roughly match our expectation:

  • Plasma cells, Monocytes/Macrophages, Dendritic cells and B cells do no have major correlation with other cell types, matches the clustering result that these 4 cell types form most distinct clusters
  • The 3 NK cell clusters are closely related with each other
  • CD8 cells, NK cells, and CD4 T cells are closely related
cormat_LE.CBSTgenes_11type = cor(LEgene_CBST_compare_expression, method = "spearman")
ggcorrplot(cormat_LE.CBSTgenes_11type)

using mean expression for filtered genes shared between SF data and LM22 matrix, over the cells with fewer zero gene expression

match1 = match(CBST.largeExpression.genes, large.express.genes.all)
LEgenes_CBST_few0_tpm = as.data.table(t(clustered_tpm_LEgenes[match1, ..fewzero_cells])) 
rownames(LEgenes_CBST_few0_tpm) = fewzero_cells
colnames(LEgenes_CBST_few0_tpm) = CBST.largeExpression.genes

LEgenes_CBST_few0_tpm[1:5,1:5]
LEgenes_CBST_few0_tpm$grouping = factor(cell_type$type[match(fewzero_cells, cell_type$cell)])
table(LEgenes_CBST_few0_tpm$grouping)
## 
##               B_cells           CD4T_memory                CD8T_B 
##                   200                   200                   200 
##                CD8T_G       Dendritic_cells Monocytes_Macrophages 
##                   200                   200                   200 
##                   NK1                   NK2                   NK3 
##                   200                   200                   200 
##          Plasma_cells                 Tregs 
##                   200                   200
# 11 x 324
LEgene_CBST_few0_expression = LEgenes_CBST_few0_tpm[, lapply(.SD, mean), by = grouping] 
LEgene_CBST_few0_expression[,1:6]
LEgene_CBST_few0_compare_expression = as.data.frame(t(LEgene_CBST_few0_expression[,-1]))
colnames(LEgene_CBST_few0_compare_expression) = LEgene_CBST_few0_expression$grouping
head(LEgene_CBST_few0_compare_expression)
rm(LEgene_CBST_few0_expression)
rm(LEgenes_CBST_few0_tpm)

The result using cells with fewer zero expression genes is not very different from the result using all cells.

cormat_LE.CBST.few0genes_11type = cor(LEgene_CBST_few0_compare_expression, 
                                      method = "spearman")
ggcorrplot(cormat_LE.CBST.few0genes_11type)

table(colnames(LEgene_CBST_few0_compare_expression) == 
        colnames(LEgene_CBST_compare_expression))
## 
## TRUE 
##   11
cor_2signatures = cor(LEgene_CBST_compare_expression, 
                      LEgene_CBST_few0_compare_expression, 
                      method = "spearman")
summary(diag(cor_2signatures))
##    Min. 1st Qu.  Median    Mean 3rd Qu.    Max. 
##  0.9358  0.9764  0.9798  0.9785  0.9887  0.9911

Correlations between our 11 cell types and the cell types in the LM22 matrix

using mean expression of filtered genes shared between SF data and LM22 matrix

cormat_LM22_11type = cor(CBST_SFLEgenes_LM22[,-1], LEgene_CBST_compare_expression,  
                         method = "spearman")
dim(cormat_LM22_11type)
## [1] 22 11
cormat_LM22_11type[1:2,1:3]
##                  B_cells CD4T_memory     CD8T_B
## B.cells.naive  0.5721863  0.07737999 0.02278787
## B.cells.memory 0.6458689  0.11158865 0.06203685
range(c(cormat_LM22_11type))
## [1] -0.3007698  0.7728268

The correlations between our 11 cell types and the cell types in the LM22 matrix still have some unexpected patterns

p = ggcorrplot(t(cormat_LM22_11type), tl.cex = 6)
p + scale_fill_gradient2(limit = c(-0.8,0.8), low = "blue", high =  "red", 
                         mid = "white", midpoint = 0)

using mean expression for filtered genes shared between SF data and LM22 matrix over cells with fewer zero gene expression

cormat_LM22_11type_few0 = cor(CBST_SFLEgenes_LM22[,-1], 
                              LEgene_CBST_few0_compare_expression,  
                         method = "spearman")
dim(cormat_LM22_11type_few0)
## [1] 22 11
cormat_LM22_11type_few0[1:2,1:3]
##                  B_cells CD4T_memory       CD8T_B
## B.cells.naive  0.5634878  0.06669643 -0.005526068
## B.cells.memory 0.6410933  0.10909850  0.040066676
range(c(cormat_LM22_11type_few0))
## [1] -0.2752991  0.7577759

The result using cells with fewer zero expression genes is not very different from the result using all cells. But we observed some potential issues: LM22 B cells seem to be correlated with all other cell types except B cells in our data.

p = ggcorrplot(t(cormat_LM22_11type_few0), tl.cex = 6)
p + scale_fill_gradient2(limit = c(-0.8,0.8), low = "blue", high =  "red", 
                         mid = "white", midpoint = 0)

Compare the scatterplot of gene expressions between LM22 cell types and our cell types

B cells and B cells

par(mar=c(3,3,2.5,0.8), mgp=c(1.5,0.5,0), cex.axis=0.85, cex.lab=0.85, cex.main=0.85)
layout(matrix(c(1,2,3,4),nrow=2,ncol = 2,byrow = TRUE))

plot(as.numeric(LEgene_CBST_compare_expression$`B_cells`), 
               as.numeric(CBST_SFLEgenes_LM22$B.cells.naive), 
               main = "Our B cell v.s. LM22 naive B cells", 
               xlab = "our B cell", ylab = "LM22 naive B cells", 
               pch=20, cex = 0.5)

plot(as.numeric(LEgene_CBST_compare_expression$`B_cells`), 
               as.numeric(CBST_SFLEgenes_LM22$B.cells.memory), 
               main = "Our B cell v.s. LM22 memory B cells", 
               xlab = "our B cell", ylab = "LM22 memory B cells", 
               pch=20, cex = 0.5)

plot(log10(as.numeric(LEgene_CBST_compare_expression$`B_cells`) + 0.01), 
               log10(as.numeric(CBST_SFLEgenes_LM22$B.cells.naive) + 0.01), 
               main = "Our B cell v.s. LM22 naive B cells, log10", 
               xlab = "our B cell", ylab = "LM22 naive B cells", 
               pch=20, cex = 0.5)

plot(log10(as.numeric(LEgene_CBST_compare_expression$`B_cells`) + 0.01), 
               log10(as.numeric(CBST_SFLEgenes_LM22$B.cells.memory) + 0.01), 
               main = "Our B cell v.s. LM22 memory B cells, log10", 
               xlab = "our B cell", ylab = "LM22 memory B cells", 
               pch=20, cex = 0.5)

CD8 cells and CD8 cells

par(mar=c(3,3,2.5,0.8), mgp=c(1.5,0.5,0), cex.axis=0.85, cex.lab=0.85, cex.main=0.85)
layout(matrix(c(1,2,3,4),nrow=2,ncol = 2,byrow = TRUE))


plot(as.numeric(LEgene_CBST_compare_expression$`CD8T_B`), 
               as.numeric(CBST_SFLEgenes_LM22$T.cells.CD8), 
               main = "CD8T_B v.s. LM22 CD8 T cells", 
               xlab = "CD8T_B", ylab = "LM22 CD8 T cells", 
               pch=20, cex = 0.5)

plot(as.numeric(LEgene_CBST_compare_expression$`CD8T_G`), 
               as.numeric(CBST_SFLEgenes_LM22$T.cells.CD8), 
               main = "CD8T_G v.s. LM22 CD8 T cells", 
               xlab = "CD8T_G", ylab = "LM22 CD8 T cells", 
               pch=20, cex = 0.5)

plot(log10(as.numeric(LEgene_CBST_compare_expression$`CD8T_B`) + 0.01), 
               log10(as.numeric(CBST_SFLEgenes_LM22$T.cells.CD8) + 0.01), 
               main = "CD8T_B v.s. LM22 CD8 T cells, log10", 
               xlab = "CD8T_B", ylab = "LM22 CD8 T cells", 
               pch=20, cex = 0.5)

plot(log10(as.numeric(LEgene_CBST_compare_expression$`CD8T_G`) + 0.01), 
               log10(as.numeric(CBST_SFLEgenes_LM22$T.cells.CD8) + 0.01), 
               main = "CD8T_G v.s. LM22 CD8 T cells, log10", 
               xlab = "CD8T_G", ylab = "LM22 CD8 T cells", 
               pch=20, cex = 0.5)

gc()
##             used   (Mb) gc trigger    (Mb) limit (Mb)   max used    (Mb)
